steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice most effectively uses information from the notes. The notes have already defined nameplate capacity and established that Hallett Wind Farm is in Australia. An effective answer must cite Hallett Wind Farm’s 351-megawatt nameplate capacity.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Hallett Wind Farm in Hallett, Australia, has a nameplate capacity of 351 megawatts.": Combines the farm's name, country, and the precise figure from the notes in one focused sentence. ✓
  • "Wind farms like Hallett Wind Farm harness the power of wind, which they convert into electricity.": Does not cite any specific figure from the notes, so it fails to use the information. ✗
  • "Hallett, Australia, is home to Hallett Wind Farm.": Mentions Hallett but drops the 351-megawatt statistic the question requires using. ✗
  • "Nameplate capacity is another name for a wind farm’s maximum electrical output.": Re-explains a term already defined and never connects it to the notes' information for this specific farm. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
